summary:
Claim: The posterior third of the tongue is drained by the posterior lymphatic chain.  
Evidence: - The posterior lymphatic chain specifically serves the posterior third of the tongue. - Central vessels drain deep structures (e.g., floor of mouth), not superficial oral surfaces.  
Answer: C
